heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: The last storm was on 02 May 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 0 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (208°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 3.8°C) and feels like temperature (down 1.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 14%) and inside humidity (up 6%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 1198 m) and light levels (up 11830 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 15780 lux), cloud levels (up 2367 m), outside temperature (up 1.6°C), and dew point temperature (down 4.0°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1198.1m lower than yesterday, 2370m lower than the past week, 1880.8m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 944m to a maximum of 4470m, averaging 885.2m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 3250m and 2770m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and calm and still conditions are expected. Cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times and weak sunlight because of clouds. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (92%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
